criteria_verification.evidence: All four models (plus ForgeConfig) defined in src/forge/config/models.py and re-exported from src/forge/config/__init__.py. TestModelsExist asserts each class exposes the expected fields via Pydantic model_fields.

criteria_verification.evidence: Defaults are pinned to the ASSUM-001..005 values via module constants DEFAULT_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_SECONDS=30, DEFAULT_STALE_HEARTBEAT_SECONDS=90, DEFAULT_CACHE_TTL_SECONDS=30, DEFAULT_INTENT_MIN_CONFIDENCE=0.7, DEFAULT_PROGRESS_INTERVAL_SECONDS=60. TestDefaultsMatchAssumptions has one test per assumption verifying both the constant and a fresh FleetConfig()/PipelineConfig() instance.

criteria_verification.evidence: ForgeConfig declares fleet=Field(default_factory=FleetConfig) and pipeline=Field(default_factory=PipelineConfig). TestForgeConfigOptionalSections.test_minimal_yaml_only_needs_permissions validates that {'permissions': {'filesystem': {'allowlist': ['/srv/forge']}}} is sufficient and the resulting fleet/pipeline carry the assumption defaults. A second test confirms default_factory hands out independent lists per instance.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: `ForgeConfig.permissions.filesystem.allowlist` is required (no default — must be explicit)
criteria_verification.evidence: FilesystemPermissions.allowlist uses Field(...) (no default), PermissionsConfig.filesystem uses Field(...) (no default), and ForgeConfig.permissions uses Field(...) (no default). TestAllowlistIsRequired exercises is_required() on the field plus three model_validate calls that each raise ValidationError when the allowlist or any wrapping block is omitted.

criteria_verification.evidence: @field_validator('allowlist') method _validate_absolute scans the list with Path.is_absolute() and raises ValueError listing the offending entries. TestAllowlistRejectsRelativePaths covers a single relative path, a mixed absolute/relative list, and a happy-path absolute-only list (positive control).
criteria_verification.notes: @field_validator('allowlist') method _validate_absolute scans the list with Path.is_absolute() and raises ValueError listing the offending entries. TestAllowlistRejectsRelativePaths covers a single relative path, a mixed absolute/relative list, and a happy-path absolute-only list (positive control).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Round-trip test: YAML → `ForgeConfig.model_validate` → back to dict preserves field values
criteria_verification.evidence: TestYamlRoundTrip.test_roundtrip_preserves_all_fields writes a fully-populated dict to a YAML file via tmp_path, loads it with yaml.safe_load, validates with ForgeConfig.model_validate, then asserts model_dump(mode='json') equals the original dict (mode='json' so Path becomes str). A second test re-validates the dumped form to ensure round-trip stability for default-only configs.
criteria_verification.notes: TestYamlRoundTrip.test_roundtrip_preserves_all_fields writes a fully-populated dict to a YAML file via tmp_path, loads it with yaml.safe_load, validates with ForgeConfig.model_validate, then asserts model_dump(mode='json') equals the original dict (mode='json' so Path becomes str).
